headline: 2 Profitable Stocks for Long-Term Investors and 1 We Brush Off
summary: Not all profitable companies are built to last - some rely on outdated models or unsustainable advantages. Just because a business is in the green today doesn’t mean it will thrive tomorrow.

content.title: 2 Profitable Stocks for Long-Term Investors and 1 We Brush Off
content.summary: Not all profitable companies are built to last - some rely on outdated models or unsustainable advantages. Just because a business is in the green today doesn’t mean it will thrive tomorrow.

headline: Onfolio and Paramount Terminate LOI Relating to Strategic Transaction
summary: Transaction’s Conditions to Closing Were Not Likely to be Met Both Parties Felt Terminating the Transaction Was in Best Interests of the Shareholders of Both Onfolio and Paramount WILMINGTON, Del., July 22, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Onfolio Holding, Inc. (Nasdaq: ONFO, ONFOW) (OTC: ONFOP) ("Onfolio" or the "Company"), an owner-operator of cash-generative online businesses, today announced the mutual termination of the previously announced binding Letter of Intent (“LOI”) with Paramount Helium LLC

headline: EUROPEAN COMMISSION APPROVES PARAMOUNT SKYDANCE CORPORATION ACQUISITION OF WARNER BROS. DISCOVERY MARKING MAJOR MILESTONE TOWARDS COMPLETION
summary: The European Commission has today formally cleared the acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery, Inc. (NASDAQ: WBD) ("WBD") by Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) ("Paramount"), representing a major milestone in completing the transaction in line with the publicly stated timeline.

content.title: EUROPEAN COMMISSION APPROVES PARAMOUNT SKYDANCE CORPORATION ACQUISITION OF WARNER BROS. DISCOVERY MARKING MAJOR MILESTONE TOWARDS COMPLETION

(NASDAQ: WBD) ("WBD") by Paramount Skydance Corporation (NASDAQ: PSKY) ("Paramount"), representing a major milestone in completing the transaction in line with the publicly stated timeline.

headline: Paramount gets EU approval for Warner Bros takeover despite US suit
summary: Investing.com -- Paramount Skydance Corp. received conditional approval from the European Union on Wednesday for its $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., marking progress for the deal as it faces legal challenges in the United States.

content.title: Paramount gets EU approval for Warner Bros takeover despite US suit
content.summary: Investing.com -- Paramount Skydance Corp. received conditional approval from the European Union on Wednesday for its $110 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., marking progress for the deal as it faces legal challenges in the United States.

headline: European Union gives its greenlight to Paramount and Warner's mega merger with some conditions
summary: The European Union approved Paramount's $81 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery this week, effectively clearing another regulatory hurdle for a mega merger that could vastly reshape the entertainment and media landscape worldwide.  The European Commission — which serves as the EU's antitrust enforcer — said that even with a Paramount-Warner combo, enough competitors would exist across markets like film production and streaming in its 27-nation bloc.  To address this, the Commission said Skydance-owned Paramount agreed to end its European Economic Area stake in United International Pictures — a longstanding venture with another major studio, Universal, that Paramount has used to distribute films in theaters outside North America.

content.title: European Union gives its greenlight to Paramount and Warner's mega merger with some conditions
content.summary: The European Union approved Paramount's $81 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery this week, effectively clearing another regulatory hurdle for a mega merger that could vastly reshape the entertainment and media landscape worldwide.  The European Commission — which serves as the EU's antitrust enforcer — said that even with a Paramount-Warner combo, enough competitors would exist across markets like film production and streaming in its 27-nation bloc.  To address this, the Commission said Skydance-owned Paramount agreed to end its European Economic Area stake in United International Pictures — a longstanding venture with another major studio, Universal, that Paramount has used to distribute films in theaters outside North America.
